description: Add per-phase gate artifacts, parallel QA phase (catches self-loops and output-schema regressions), reusable fidelity/authority prompt module, LLM critic wrappers over deterministic contradictions/narrative tools, and self-critique (v1 → surgical v2) on new LLM callsites. All new LLM features default-off behind Settings flags. Do NOT replace PhaseOrchestrator, do NOT migrate to claude-agent-sdk, do NOT wrap merge_paths/clarification/generate.py.

userStories.title: Add phase_artifacts storage helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a helper that writes/reads per-phase JSON artifacts next to the monolithic project JSON so subsequent phases (and QA validators) can read specific phase output.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/storage/phase_artifacts.py

Expose: async def write_phase_artifact(project_id: str, phase: str, payload: dict) -> Path — writes to {Settings().LOCAL_DATA_DIR}/projects/{Settings().FIRESTORE_COLLECTION}/{project_id}/phases/{phase}.json; creates parent dirs; uses asyncio.to_thread for the write
Expose: async def read_phase_artifact(project_id: str, phase: str) -> dict | None — returns parsed JSON or None if absent
Expose: def phase_artifact_exists(project_id: str, phase: str) -> bool — sync existence check
Expose: def completed_phases(project_id: str) -> list[str] — returns phases in PHASE_ORDER (import from orchestrator or re-declare) whose artifact files exist, in PHASE_ORDER sequence

Create backend/tests/test_phase_artifacts.py with: (1) write+read roundtrip, (2) read_phase_artifact returns None when missing, (3) phase_artifact_exists False when missing, (4) completed_phases returns phases in correct order
Use a pytest tmp_path fixture or monkeypatch Settings().LOCAL_DATA_DIR to avoid touching real data dir
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Wire artifact writes into orchestrator phase methods
userStories.description: As a developer, I need each pipeline phase method to write its own structured artifact at completion so subsequent phases and QA validators can read the phase output directly.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify backend/src/walkthrough/ai/orchestrator.py
Import write_phase_artifact from walkthrough.storage.phase_artifacts
At end of _run_path_merge: write 'path_merge' artifact with {"decision_trees": [t.model_dump(mode='json') for t in project.decision_trees]}
At end of _run_narrative: write 'narrative' artifact with same shape (trees now carry narratives)
At end of _run_contradictions: write 'contradictions' artifact with {"gaps": [g.model_dump(mode='json') for g in project.gaps]}
At end of _run_clarification: write 'clarification' artifact with {"questions": [q.model_dump(mode='json') for q in project.questions]}
Inside _run_generation, after generate_walkthrough returns: write 'generation' artifact with the full output dict
Do NOT write an ingestion artifact (per-video/PDF id checks already cover resume)
Keep existing self._save(project) calls untouched (monolith stays authoritative)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass (existing tests must still pass)

userStories.title: Replace _infer_resume_phase with artifact-presence lookup

acceptanceCriteria: Modify backend/src/walkthrough/ai/orchestrator.py::_infer_resume_phase
Keep the status='complete' → 'done' branch and status='generating' → 'generation' branch exactly as today
Keep the status='clarifying' branch exactly as today (unresolved critical → 'clarification', else 'generation')

For the 'analyzing' / 'uploading' path: use completed_phases(project.project_id) to find the first phase in PHASE_ORDER whose artifact is absent. Fall back to 'ingestion' when no artifacts exist or project.videos + project.pdfs are both empty.
Import completed_phases from walkthrough.storage.phase_artifacts

Create backend/tests/test_orchestrator_resume.py with cases: (1) artifacts through narrative.json → returns 'contradictions', (2) artifacts through contradictions.json + status 'clarifying' + unresolved critical gaps → returns 'clarification', (3) no artifacts + empty project → returns 'ingestion', (4) status 'complete' → returns 'done'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

ValidatorFinding(BaseModel): severity: Literal['critical','medium','low','info']; code: str; message: str; screen_id: str|None = None; evidence: list[SourceRef] = []
ValidatorResult(BaseModel): validator: str; ok: bool; findings: list[ValidatorFinding] = []
QAReport(BaseModel): project_id: str; results: list[ValidatorResult]; has_critical: bool; generated_at: datetime
Import SourceRef from walkthrough.models.workflow

userStories.title: Add decision_tree_structure validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure-Python validator that catches structural issues in decision trees — specifically the self-loop bug and related defects — before they reach the UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/qa/__init__.py (empty)
Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/qa/decision_tree_structure.py
Expose async def validate(project: Project) -> ValidatorResult
Detect 'self_loop' (severity critical): any branch where branch.paths[action] == branch.screen_id
Detect 'orphan_screen' (severity medium): screen that is not root and not any branch-path target in its tree
Detect 'unreachable_screen' (severity medium): BFS from tree.root_screen_id fails to reach the screen
Detect 'dangling_branch_target' (severity critical): branch path points at a screen_id absent from tree.screens
Each finding sets screen_id and a descriptive message
Return ValidatorResult(validator='decision_tree_structure', ok=not any(critical findings), findings=[...])
Create backend/tests/test_qa_decision_tree.py with fixture Projects producing each finding; include one test reproducing the known self-loop at screen_d3af07fa2d51
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add output_schema validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure-Python validator that catches the generated-output field-name regressions (elements/ui_elements, stat field names) that broke the walkthrough page in the current session.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/qa/output_schema.py
Expose async def validate(project: Project) -> ValidatorResult

Load phases/generation.json via read_phase_artifact; if missing, return ValidatorResult(validator='output_schema', ok=False, findings=[ValidatorFinding(severity='critical', code='generation_artifact_missing', message='...')])
Detect 'missing_screen_field' (critical): screen dict missing screen_id, title, ui_elements, evidence_tier, or source_refs
Detect 'legacy_elements_key' (critical): screen has 'elements' key (regression for bug the user just fixed — generate.py should write 'ui_elements')
Detect 'bad_ui_element' (critical): element missing element_type or label
Detect 'warning_references_missing_screen' (medium): warnings[i].screen_id not in screens
Detect 'stats_field_mismatch' (critical): stats missing total_branches or open_questions, OR stats contains legacy 'total_branch_points' / 'open_questions_count' / 'warnings_count'
Create backend/tests/test_qa_output_schema.py with fixture artifacts for each finding
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add video_coverage validator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure-Python validator ensuring every observed screen has video evidence and every screen has at least one source reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/qa/video_coverage.py
Expose async def validate(project: Project) -> ValidatorResult
Iterate every WorkflowScreen across all decision_trees
Detect 'observed_without_video_ref' (critical): evidence_tier == 'observed' but no source_refs with source_type == 'video'
Detect 'screen_without_any_ref' (critical): len(source_refs) == 0
Return ValidatorResult with validator='video_coverage'
Create backend/tests/test_qa_video_coverage.py covering each finding
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add narrative_fidelity_critic (LLM validator, flag-gated)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an LLM validator that checks whether each screen's narrative claims are actually supported by its cited source excerpts, to catch hallucinated narrative.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Settings.QA_ENABLE_LLM_CRITIC: bool = False and Settings.NARRATIVE_FIDELITY_MODEL: str = 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001' to backend/src/walkthrough/config.py
Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/qa/narrative_fidelity_critic.py
Expose async def validate(project: Project, *, client: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic | None = None) -> ValidatorResult
When Settings().QA_ENABLE_LLM_CRITIC is False: skip LLM calls, return ValidatorResult(validator='narrative_fidelity', ok=True, findings=[])
When True: iterate screens that have narrative; for each, build payload of {narrative: {what, why, when_condition}, excerpts: [{reference, excerpt} for r in source_refs]}

System prompt: 'You check if narrative text is supported by source excerpts. A claim is unsupported if no excerpt contains evidence for it. Paraphrase alone is NOT evidence — require overlap of specific nouns or UI labels. Output JSON: {"unsupported_claims": [{"claim": str, "reason": str}]}'
Use messages.create with model=Settings().NARRATIVE_FIDELITY_MODEL, max_tokens=1024, temperature=0.0
Each unsupported claim becomes ValidatorFinding(severity='medium', code='narrative_unsupported_claim', screen_id=..., message=claim + ' — ' + reason)
Create backend/tests/test_qa_narrative_fidelity.py with mocked AsyncAnthropic: (1) flag off returns empty without HTTP call, (2) flag on with mocked response emits findings
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add QA runner fan-out
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a runner that invokes all QA validators in parallel and serializes a QAReport artifact.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/qa/runner.py
Expose async def run_qa(project: Project) -> QAReport
Collect validator callables: decision_tree_structure.validate, output_schema.validate, video_coverage.validate, narrative_fidelity_critic.validate
Run via asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=True
For any validator that raised: coerce to ValidatorResult(validator=<name>, ok=False, findings=[ValidatorFinding(severity='critical', code='validator_error', message=str(exc))])
has_critical = any(f.severity == 'critical' for r in results for f in r.findings)
Write phases/qa.json via write_phase_artifact(project.project_id, 'qa', report.model_dump(mode='json'))
Return QAReport(project_id=project.project_id, results=results, has_critical=has_critical, generated_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc))
Create backend/tests/test_qa_runner.py: (1) all validators ok → report.has_critical False; (2) one validator raises → structured error finding; (3) artifact file written
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add _run_qa phase to orchestrator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a new 'qa' phase after generation that runs the parallel validators and stashes the report on project.walkthrough_output so the frontend can surface findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify backend/src/walkthrough/ai/orchestrator.py

Add async def _run_qa(self, project: Project) -> AsyncGenerator[ProgressEvent, None]: yields ProgressEvent('qa', 0, 'Running QA validators...'), calls run_qa(project), stashes report.model_dump(mode='json') on project.walkthrough_output['qa_report'], yields ProgressEvent('qa', 100, f'QA complete — {sum(1 for r in report.results for f in r.findings if f.severity=="critical")} critical findings')
Wire _run_qa inside run_pipeline: run after _run_generation when _phase_index(resume) <= _phase_index('qa')

Respect QA_BLOCK_ON_CRITICAL: if True and report.has_critical, set project.status = 'qa_blocked' instead of 'complete' (add 'qa_blocked' to the Project status Literal in backend/src/walkthrough/models/project.py)
Call self._save(project) after _run_qa
Create backend/tests/test_orchestrator_qa_phase.py: run an end-to-end fixture project through QA phase, assert qa.json written and project.walkthrough_output['qa_report'] populated

userStories.title: Create fidelity prompt module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable prompt-blocks module so every future LLM callsite composes its system prompt from the same invariants and rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/prompts/__init__.py (empty)
Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/prompts/fidelity.py exporting four string constants
FIDELITY_STANDARD — hard rules: no inference; quoted excerpts only; if source is unreadable, return literal '__unreadable__' and stop; reference the evidence explicitly
INVARIANTS — copy M1-M9 and N1-N7 verbatim from backend/src/walkthrough/ai/agent.py:40-76

AUTHORITY_HIERARCHY — state: 'Video (tier 1, direct observation) > SOP PDF (tier 2, authoritative documentation) > training narration (tier 3, instructor commentary). This hierarchy guides evidence weighting and UI presentation order ONLY. Do NOT silently resolve disagreements (see N4 / N2). Surface them as Gaps with evidence from at least two sources.'
EVIDENCE_CITATION_RULES — every claim ties to a SourceRef; excerpts ≤200 chars; no paraphrasing in citations; citations include source_type + reference + excerpt

Create backend/tests/test_prompts.py asserting each constant is non-empty, is str, contains key markers ('authority' in AUTHORITY_HIERARCHY; '__unreadable__' in FIDELITY_STANDARD; 'M1' in INVARIANTS; 'SourceRef' in EVIDENCE_CITATION_RULES)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Create deterministic prompt composer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that composes system prompts from fidelity blocks in a deterministic order so prompt caching works.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/prompts/compose.py

def compose_system_prompt(*blocks: str, task: str) -> str — joins blocks with '

---

', appends task at the end prefixed by '

## Task

'
Raises ValueError if task is empty or whitespace-only
Same inputs always produce byte-identical output (no timestamps, no random content)
Does NOT accept dynamic payload content — dynamic content belongs in the user message for prompt-cache stability
Extend backend/tests/test_prompts.py: (1) compose same args twice → identical strings; (2) empty task → ValueError; (3) composed output contains every block plus the task
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

Preserve existing SYSTEM_PROMPT and class definitions exactly — other code may still harvest them
Verify file still imports: python -c 'import walkthrough.ai.agent' succeeds (if run via uv run)

userStories.title: Create shared Anthropic client helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single Anthropic client factory with a structured-JSON helper so every critic shares the same retry, caching, and schema-validation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/llm/__init__.py (empty)
Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/llm/client.py
def get_client() -> anthropic.AsyncAnthropic — reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from Settings; raises if unset only when actually called (module import must not fail)

async def run_structured_json(client, *, model: str, system: str, user: str, schema: type[BaseModel], max_tokens: int = 4096, max_retries: int = 2) -> BaseModel — calls messages.create with system=[{type: 'text', text: system, cache_control: {type: 'ephemeral'}}] for prompt caching; extracts the text content; attempts schema.model_validate_json; on failure, retries up to max_retries with a reminder message 'Return ONLY valid JSON per the schema; no markdown fencing'; raises ValueError if exhausted

userStories.title: Create detect_contradictions_critic (LLM)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an LLM critic that reads existing deterministic gaps plus raw source data and emits additional contradictions the heuristic detector may have missed.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Settings.CONTRADICTION_CRITIC_MODEL: str = 'claude-sonnet-4-6' to backend/src/walkthrough/config.py

Expose async def critique_contradictions(videos: list[VideoAnalysis], pdfs: list[PDFExtraction], decision_trees: list[DecisionTree], existing_gaps: list[Gap]) -> list[Gap]

Build system prompt via compose_system_prompt(FIDELITY_STANDARD, INVARIANTS, AUTHORITY_HIERARCHY, EVIDENCE_CITATION_RULES, task='Compare UI labels across videos, PDF sections, and audio excerpts. Identify contradictions the deterministic detector may have missed. Output JSON: {"gaps": [{"gap_id": str, "severity": "critical|medium|low", "description": str, "evidence": [SourceRef-like dicts from >=2 sources]}]}')
User payload: compact JSON of video UI labels per timestamp, PDF section titles + excerpts, audio transcript segments, and existing_gaps (so the LLM avoids duplicating)
Use run_structured_json with a LLMGapsResponse Pydantic model (gaps: list[Gap])
Post-process: reuse _deduplicate_gaps from backend/src/walkthrough/ai/tools/detect_contradictions.py (import it) to filter duplicates against existing_gaps
If QA_ENABLE_LLM_CRITIC is False, return empty list without making any LLM call

When flag is False: behavior identical to before this PR

userStories.title: Create surgical_review helper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable 'v1 → surgical v2' review helper so LLM critic output can be self-checked idempotently.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Settings.ENABLE_SELF_CRITIQUE: bool = False and Settings.SELF_CRITIQUE_MODEL: str = 'claude-sonnet-4-6' to backend/src/walkthrough/config.py
Create backend/src/walkthrough/ai/llm/review_pass.py
async def surgical_review(client: anthropic.AsyncAnthropic, *, model: str, v1_output: dict, source_excerpts: list[str], schema: type[BaseModel]) -> dict

System prompt: review v1 for unsupported claims, missing citations, hallucinated labels; output JSON {edits: [{path: str, operation: 'replace'|'remove'|'add', new_value: any}]}; empty edits list means v1 is already correct (idempotence)
Apply edits to a deep-copy of v1_output using simple dotted-path logic; validate against schema; return v2
When ENABLE_SELF_CRITIQUE is False, return v1_output unchanged

Create backend/tests/test_review_pass.py: (1) flag off → v1 unchanged; (2) flag on with mocked response emitting edits → v2 differs; (3) running surgical_review on v2 with same mock returning empty edits → v3 == v2 (idempotence)

Tests pass

Pass source_excerpts built from the same payload the v1 call received
